hints.Indicative: Indicative present would be 'facis' — 'fac' is the irregular imperative singular of 'facere'
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'facias' — 'fac' is the imperative singular
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'facere' — 'fac' is the imperative singular

hints.ago, agere, egi, actum: These are the principal parts of 'agere' (to do/drive)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the principal parts of 'dare' (to give)
hints.dico, dicere, dixi, dictum: These are the principal parts of 'dicere' (to say)

steps.prompt: What form is 'flere' in this sentence?

hints.Present indicative: Present indicative 3rd sg. would be 'flet' — 'flere' is the infinitive form used with 'fac'
hints.Imperative: Imperative singular would be 'fle' — 'flere' is the infinitive complement of 'fac'
hints.Gerund: A gerund would be 'flendum' — 'flere' is the infinitive complement of 'fac'

hints.fluo, fluere, fluxi, fluxum: These are the principal parts of 'fluere' (to flow)
hints.gemo, gemere, gemui, gemitum: These are the principal parts of 'gemere' (to groan)
hints.doleo, dolere, dolui, dolitum: These are the principal parts of 'dolere' (to grieve)

translation: Make me weep devoutly with you
